title: Breastfeeding Calorie Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: How Many Extra Calories Do You Need While Breastfeeding?
description: Estimate extra calories needed while nursing based on nursing type and activity. Free lactation nutrition calculator for postpartum weight management.
intro: Breastfeeding requires extra energy to produce milk: between **300 and 500 additional kcal per day**. This calculator estimates your total daily calorie needs while nursing based on your weight, activity level, and how you're feeding your baby.
keyTakeaway: Exclusive breastfeeding requires **~500 extra kcal** per day. A 65 kg woman with moderate activity needs **~2,200–2,500 kcal/day** while nursing. **Don't restrict calories** while breastfeeding.
useCases: You're breastfeeding and want to know how much to eat.
You want to lose postpartum weight without reducing milk supply.
You need to plan your nutrition while nursing.
Your nutritionist asked you to calculate extra calorie needs.
You want to know how much energy breast milk production requires.

dataUpdate.notes: IOM and WHO recommendations for nutrition during breastfeeding.

example.title: Example: 65 kg, light activity, exclusive breastfeeding
example.result: You need approximately **2,466 kcal/day** while exclusively breastfeeding. **Don't drop below 1,800 kcal** to maintain milk production.

explanation:
## Extra Calories by Nursing Type

| Type | Extra kcal/day |
|---|---|
| Exclusive breastfeeding | 400–500 kcal |
| Mixed feeding | 250–350 kcal |
| Partial nursing | 100–200 kcal |

## Where Do the Extra Calories Come From?

Producing 750–800 ml of breast milk daily requires ~500 kcal. Part comes from your diet and part from fat reserves accumulated during pregnancy (which is why breastfeeding helps with postpartum weight loss).

## Key Nutrients While Nursing

- **Calcium**: 1,000 mg/day (dairy, leafy greens)
- **Iron**: 9–15 mg/day
- **Vitamin D**: 600–1,000 IU/day
- **Omega-3 (DHA)**: 200–300 mg/day (fish)
- **Water**: 2.5–3 liters/day

## Can I Diet While Breastfeeding?

Restrictive diets (< 1,800 kcal) are not recommended while nursing. A moderate calorie deficit of 300–500 kcal is safe for losing ~0.5 kg/week without affecting milk supply.

## Related Calculators

- [Postpartum Weight Loss](/postpartum-weight-loss) — weight tracking.
- [Breast Milk Amount](/breast-milk-formula) — per feeding.
- [Breast Pump Output](/breast-pump-output) — milk production.

faq.q: How many extra calories do I need while breastfeeding?
faq.a: Exclusive breastfeeding requires **400–500 extra kcal** per day. Mixed feeding needs ~250–350 kcal extra. You shouldn't eat for two, but don't restrict either.

faq.q: Does breastfeeding help you lose weight?
faq.a: Yes, producing milk burns **~500 kcal/day**. Some of that energy comes from fat stored during pregnancy. Many women naturally lose weight while nursing without dieting.
A **moderate** calorie deficit (300–500 kcal below your needs) is safe. But don't drop below **1,800 kcal/day**. Very restrictive diets can reduce milk production and harm your health.

faq.q: How much water should I drink while breastfeeding?
faq.a: Aim for at least **2.5–3 liters per day**. Drink a glass of water whenever you nurse. Dehydration can reduce milk supply.

faq.q: Are there foods I should avoid while breastfeeding?
faq.a: No foods are universally prohibited. Limit **caffeine** to 200–300 mg/day (2–3 cups of coffee), **avoid alcohol** (or wait 2 hours after drinking), and limit high-mercury fish.
No, moderate exercise **doesn't affect** milk quality or quantity. Nurse or pump before exercising for comfort. Wear a supportive sports bra.

faq.q: Do I need supplements while breastfeeding?
faq.a: Continue **vitamin D** (600–1,000 IU/day) and **folic acid** (500 mcg/day). If your diet is varied, you don't need additional supplements. If vegetarian or vegan, ask about B12 and iron.

sources.name: IOM — Dietary Reference Intakes for Energy
